summary: Israeli security forces launched extensive searches in the West Bank village of Muhmas after Israeli civilians lost contact, while a Hamas platoon commander was killed in southern Gaza and a reported drone strike hit a vehicle in Nabatieh, South Lebanon. The U.S. and Iran are preparing for a next negotiation round on July 18 with Washington issuing warnings over Hormuz Strait activity, and a Tajik national was arrested in Israel for allegedly spying for Iranian intelligence during the "Roar of the Lion" operation.
key_points: IDF and police conducting wide-area scans in Muhmas village, Benjamin Brigade, after report of Israeli civilians losing contact in the area. (Source: @idf_telegram/24491)

Hamas platoon commander Adel Jihad Mohammad Asfour killed in an aerial strike in southern Gaza; IDF says he advanced sniper and explosive attacks and worked to rebuild capabilities. (Source: @manniefabian/58720)
Reported drone strike targeting a vehicle in Nabatieh, South Lebanon. (Source: @New_security8200/202184)
Warplanes reported flying toward Beirut with maximum alert declared. (Source: @redlinkleb/29469)
Next U.S.-Iran negotiation round scheduled for July 18 per Saudi Al-Hadath source; U.S. warned Iran against changing Hormuz Strait status quo. (Source: @N12chat/215632)
U.S. messages to Iran via Saudi channel: Hormuz behavior is first test of MOU commitment; escalation there would directly affect understandings. (Source: @yediotnews25/132185)

Iran, Qatar, Pakistan held two meetings on Islamabad; urgent monitoring channel to form by tomorrow; discussed spending part of $6B held in Qatar for approved Iranian purchases. (Source: @shin_persian/7321)

Tajik national with Russian passport arrested in Israel for allegedly documenting Iranian missile impacts, photographing Azrieli Towers, Haifa Port, and attempting to film a sensitive northern facility during "Roar of the Lion." (Source: @inon_yttach/9248)

sections.executive_summary: Israeli security forces launched extensive searches in the West Bank village of Muhmas after Israeli civilians lost contact, while a Hamas platoon commander was killed in southern Gaza and a reported drone strike hit a vehicle in Nabatieh, South Lebanon. The U.S. and Iran are preparing for a next negotiation round on July 18 with Washington issuing warnings over Hormuz Strait activity, and a Tajik national was arrested in Israel for allegedly spying for Iranian intelligence during the "Roar of the Lion" operation. Warplanes were reported flying toward Beirut under maximum alert, signaling heightened northern tension.

points: Iran accused the U.S. of violating MOU commitments and agreed to establish an emergency contact channel by tomorrow; discussions with Qatar and Pakistan covered U.S. force buildup, threats, and using part of $6B in frozen assets for approved purchases. (Source: @pkpoi/143096)

Iran, Qatar, and Pakistan held two meetings today on Islamabad; an urgent monitoring channel will be formed by tomorrow; Qatar says the $6B has not been transferred to Tehran. (Source: @shin_persian/7321)

Kurdish Democratic Party secretary-general Mustafa Hijri stated a ground invasion of Iran is discussed but advancing to Tehran is not on the agenda; if conditions permit, they would advance to Urmia. (Source: @kianmeli1/88249)
An Iranian MP claimed a coup is underway against Mojtaba Khamenei. (Source: @kianmeli1/88254)

Three teachers' union activists received prison sentences and supplementary punishments; another union activist arrested in Fars province as judicial and security pressure on labor activists intensifies. (Source: @VahidOnline/76762)

IDF and police launched extensive scans in Muhmas village, Benjamin Brigade, after a report that several Israeli civilians were in the village and lost contact; two were reached after four hours, others believed to have left independently. (Source: @inon_yttach/9245)

Hamas platoon commander Adel Jihad Mohammad Asfour killed in an aerial strike in southern Gaza; IDF says he advanced sniper and explosive attacks and recently worked to rebuild Hamas capabilities. (Source: @manniefabian/58720)

Tajik national with Russian passport arrested in June for Iranian intelligence tasks during "Roar of the Lion": documented missile impact locations, transmitted Azrieli Towers coordinates, photographed Haifa Port, attempted to film a sensitive northern facility, and recruited others. (Source: @inon_yttach/9248)
New Battalion 52 commander inaugurated replacing Lt. Col. Dor Ben Simhon who fell in Lebanon; emphasized resilience, continuity, and victory. (Source: @inon_yttach/9249)

Northern communities beginning rehabilitation: growth neighborhoods started in Dafna, Yiftah, Misgav Am, Admit, Zarit; future neighborhoods planned for Manara, Dishon, Kfar Yuval, Margaliot; renovations in Beit Hillel, Yiron, Malkia, Ein Baruch, Rosh Hanikra; total ~300 families. (Source: @inon_yttach/9239)
Netanyahu coalition advancing amended election law via fast-track "mahat" procedure in Constitution Committee chaired by Simcha Rothman. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373953)
U.S. Triton drone completed mission over Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman and returned to base. (Source: @shin_persian/7325)

Hamas platoon commander Adel Jihad Mohammad Asfour killed in an aerial strike in southern Gaza; IDF says he advanced sniper and explosive attacks and recently worked to rebuild Hamas capabilities. (Source: @manniefabian/58720)

IDF and police conducting wide-area scans in Muhmas village, Benjamin Brigade, after report of Israeli civilians losing contact; two contacted after four hours, others believed to have left independently. (Source: @inon_yttach/9245)
IDF reported blowing up buildings in Beit Hanoun; message describes location as South Lebanon but Beit Hanoun is in northern Gaza. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93325)
Hamas attempting to rebuild capabilities after 1,000 days of war per Israeli analysis. (Source: @amitsegal/62706)
Reported drone strike targeting a vehicle in Nabatieh, South Lebanon. (Source: @New_security8200/202184)
Warplanes reported flying toward Beirut with maximum alert declared. (Source: @redlinkleb/29469)
Lebanese President Aoun said Syrian FM visit to Lebanon today removes fears of Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa intending to intervene in Lebanon. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373955)
IDF engineering corps training exercise described as "most explosive series" for new engineers. (Source: @idf_telegram/24487)
Turkish forces arrested six Kurdish youths in Shiladze village in Iraqi Kurdistan and released them after a day of alleged torture. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80560)
Iraqi journalist Haider al-Hamdani detained in Karbala after complaint from Abbasid shrine. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80559)
Press conference scheduled Sunday or Monday to announce details for Khamenei funeral ceremonies in Iraq; meetings ongoing with all relevant parties. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80551)
Dedicated channel created for central media committee for Khamenei funeral in Iraq. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80553)

Saudi Al-Hadath published U.S. messages to Iran: any attempt to change Hormuz Strait status quo unacceptable; Iranian behavior in Hormuz is first test of MOU commitment; U.S. monitoring activity; escalation in Hormuz would have direct consequences on understandings. (Source: @yediotnews25/132185)
Next U.S.-Iran negotiation round scheduled for July 18 per senior source to Saudi Al-Hadath. (Source: @N12chat/215632)
Qatar hosting $6B in Iranian assets; discussed spending part via approved goods purchases for Iran; Qatar says funds not transferred to Tehran. (Source: @shin_persian/7321)

key_developments: IDF and police conducting wide-area scans in Muhmas village, Benjamin Brigade, after report of Israeli civilians losing contact; two contacted after four hours. (Source: @inon_yttach/9245)
Hamas platoon commander Adel Jihad Mohammad Asfour killed in aerial strike in southern Gaza; advanced sniper and explosive attacks, rebuilding capabilities. (Source: @manniefabian/58720)
Reported drone strike on vehicle in Nabatieh, South Lebanon. (Source: @New_security8200/202184)
Warplanes flying toward Beirut, maximum alert declared. (Source: @redlinkleb/29469)
Next U.S.-Iran negotiation round set for July 18; U.S. warns Hormuz Strait status quo changes unacceptable, Iranian behavior there is first MOU test. (Source: @N12chat/215632, @yediotnews25/132185)
Iran, Qatar, Pakistan held two meetings on Islamabad; urgent monitoring channel by tomorrow; discussed spending part of $6B in Qatar for approved Iranian purchases. (Source: @shin_persian/7321)

Tajik national with Russian passport arrested in Israel for Iranian intelligence tasks during "Roar of the Lion": documented missile impacts, photographed Azrieli Towers and Haifa Port, attempted to film sensitive northern facility. (Source: @inon_yttach/9248)
sections.threat_assessment: The simultaneous West Bank search operation, Gaza targeted killing, and reported Lebanese drone strike indicate a multi-front operational tempo. The Beirut warplane alert suggests imminent Israeli aerial activity over Lebanon, possibly preemptive or retaliatory. U.S.-Iran diplomatic track remains active but fragile; Hormuz Strait warnings signal red lines that could trigger escalation if Iranian naval behavior shifts. The arrested Tajik operative reveals an Iranian intelligence network actively mapping Israeli strategic targets during recent conflict, implying advanced preparation for future operations. Northern community rehabilitation proceeds alongside military posture, reflecting Israeli confidence in border security but also long-term settlement intent.
sections.regional_response: Lebanon's president welcomed the Syrian FM visit as reducing intervention fears, signaling Beirut's desire to contain spillover. Iraq hosts Khamenei funeral preparations with high-level coordination, reflecting Iranian influence depth. Qatar mediates financial channel for Iranian assets while denying transfer to Tehran, balancing U.S. and Iranian equities. Saudi media amplifies U.S. messaging to Iran, aligning Riyadh with Washington's pressure track. Turkish operations in Iraqi Kurdistan continue targeting Kurdish elements, complicating Baghdad-Ankara-Erbil dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Muhmas incident began as a traffic violation chase involving Breslov Hasidim who entered the village, were detained and released, then called for help two hours later — suggesting either a staged incident or rapid escalation by local actors. The "Roar of the Lion" reference in the Tajik spy case confirms an Iranian operational code name for recent missile activity, providing a hook for SIGINT correlation. The Kurdish Democratic Party's stated willingness to advance to Urmia if conditions permit indicates cross-border militant coordination that could exploit Iranian internal instability. The fast-track Israeli election law amendment may signal domestic political preparation for wartime governance changes.
